design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Counterfactual-fairness CPDAG orientation witness. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Kusner-Loftus-Russell-Silva counterfactual fairness, Chiappa/Zhang-Bareinboim path-specific counterfactual fairness, Shpitser-style path-specific identification with recanting witnesses, and recent counterfactual fairness with imperfect causal graphs. Why non-trivial? The target is not enumerating every DAG in a Markov equivalence class and applying a known ID algorithm: require a minimal CPDAG witness class where all compatible DAGs agree on the total protected-attribute effect and observed Markov law, but disagree on the path-specific unfair effect because a single compelled/uncompelled orientation crosses the fair/unfair path partition. The theorem should give a concrete orientation-cut certificate and an if-and-only-if invariance phase for when a path-specific fairness estimand is DAG-invariant over the equivalence class. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a graph-theoretic non-equivalence certificate: two Markov-equivalent DAGs with identical observational factorization and total-effect identifiability but distinct unfair-path counterfactuals, plus a hand-checkable orientation-cut normal form. If the delta reduces to CPDAG enumeration, recanting-witness criterion reuse, support coverage, or definition-unfold fairness invariance, pivot or accept field-tier.

banked_reason: Stage -1.1 rejected the topic as out-of-scope: the CPDAG counterfactual-fairness/path-specific orientation certificate lives in fairness and graphical-model equivalence-class reasoning rather than the allowed econometric identification, partial-identification, panel, or causal-ML orthogonality clusters.

seeds_burned.reason: Out-of-scope at Stage -1.1; do not retry fairness/path-specific CPDAG orientation witnesses in thmsmith unless the cluster policy changes.
